MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard: PES-18-301736 Estate of Helena Chu and
PES-18-302322 Estate of Roy Chu.

Appearances: Michael Dobrov appeared by zoom for Brian Chu (executor).

Mr. Dobrov stated that he sent the inventory and appraisal to the probate referee, that he is working on the accounting petition and waiting on some bank statements. The Petitions for Order Approving Status Report on Appointment of Guardian of the Estate of Minor, and Status on close of estates are ordered off calendar in both cases. The First Accounts and Reports of Executor, etc., are taken under submission with the intent to settle the account as presented in both cases.

Judge: Stephen Murphy: Clerk: Ateesh Datt: Deputy: Clark: Not reported.

Petition For Order Approving Status Report On Appointment Of Guardian Of Estate Of Minor
Off Calendar May-30-2023 Continued To Jun-20-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-18-301736 Estate of Helena Chu and
PES-18-302322 Estate of Roy Chu.

Appearances: Michael Dobrov appeared by zoom for Brian Chu (executor).
Nicholas Jay appeared by zoom for Willard Ping (proposed guardian of the estate of Jenna Wong).

The court noted that this was previously on calendar to get a guardian appointed. Mr. Jay stated that he drafted a petition, that he is waiting for approval of the petition from the parents and their attorney, and that he will file it within a week from receiving consent of the parents. Mr. Dobrov stated that having a blocked account is the best way to go as it is the easiest way and the least expensive. The court reminded Mr. Dobrov that he needs to clear the examiner's notes. Mr. Dobrov stated that he filed the inventory and appraisal, but it got bounced back because it needs to be signed by the probate referee. The Petitions for Order Approving Status Report on Appointment of Guardian of the Estate of Minor, the First Accounts and Reports of Executor, etc., and status on close of estates are continued to June 20, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Jay is to file his petitions one week prior.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Probate Examiner: Aeyoung Kim. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

Petition For Order Approving Status Report On Appointment Of Guardian Of Estate Of Minor
Off Calendar Apr-11-2023 Continued To May-30-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard; PES-18-301736 The Estate of Helena Chiu and PES-18-302322 Estate of Roy Chu.

Appearances; Michael Dobrov appearing via zoom for Brian Chu (Executor).

Mr. Dobrov stated that Mr. Ping has not been appointed as the Guardian of the estate of the minor beneficiary (Jenna Wong), and that Nicolas Jay is working with the parents and with counsel for Natalie Wong. Mr. Dubrov also stated Natalie has returned the money in full that he submitted a declaration in both estates and that the declarations addressed some of the issues that were raised in the examiner's notes. In addition, Mr. Dubrov stated that the money for Roy's estate will be credited in the next accounting, that he will be submitting a second and third accounting, that the second accounting will show nothing and that his client is instructed to move the assets from New York to California because New York has no jurisdiction. Furthermore Mr. Dobrov stated that he agrees that the money for Jenna should be places in a blocked account and asked for a date in the end of May/early June. The petitions for order approving status report (2), the first account and report of executor, etc,. (2), and status on close of estate (2) are continued to May 30, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel is to go thru the examiner's notes and get everything filed before May 30th.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: Ateesh Datt. Deputy: Svahlin. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The First Accounting
Off Calendar Jun-21-2022 Continued To Jul-12-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Dobrov, via Zoom, for Brian Chu, Executor.

Counsel reports of working on an accounting and that net proceeds of the sale of the estate's real property have been deposited in a bank in New York, half of which was deposited into Helena Chu's estate account and the remaining half into Roy Chu's estate account which is controlled by his sister Natalie. Counsel reports that he has spoken with a New York attorney who advised him that they are working on an accounting for the probate matter in that jurisdiction and that there is movement on appointing a Guardian Ad Litem ("GAL") for one of Natalie's children. The court advises counsel that if there is no progress regarding the GAL issue by the next calling of this matter, counsel will need to look to appoint a professional.

The matter is continued to Jul-12-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The First Accounting
Off Calendar May-10-2022 Continued To Jun-21-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Dobrov, via Zoom, for and with Brian Chu, Personal Representative; Rosa Martinez-Genzon, via Zoom, for SureTec Insurance Co., Surety for the Estate of Roy Chu.

The court notes the declarations filed in the related matters and asks for an update regarding the New York Probate. Counsel offers testimony regarding the preceding and advises that Natalie Wong is working to secure a loan to return the funds that have been distributed without court approval. The court confirms with counsel as to the dates that the two-year accounting should cover.

The Status on Filing of First Accounting is continued to Jun-21-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Counsel is instructed to file the accounting by Jun-14-2022.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The First Accounting
Off Calendar Apr-12-2022 Continued To May-10-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-15-2022 Off Calendar

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Feb-17-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-18-301736 The Estate of Helena Chu and
PES-18-303233 The Estate of Roy Chu.

Appearances: Michael Dobrov appeared by zoom for and with Brian Chu (executor) by zoom.
Rosa Martinez-Genzon appeared by zoom for Suretec Insurance Company but only in the Roy Chu estate.

The court explained what the FDIC limits are for estate funds that are being held in bank accounts, noted that the funds that were allocated to Roy's estate were deposited into a bank in New York outside of its jurisdiction, that the account is connected to the estate proceeding that is in New York, not these proceedings, that they largely disappeared, and that its understanding is that the money was removed from that account by Natalie (Brian's sister) and she refuses to restore the funds. The court also stated that the reference in the petition that this court issue an order to the San Francisco branch of ICBC freezing what remains in the New York State account is completely unhelpful because it does not have any jurisdiction over the bank account in New York, and noted that if the insured limits had been followed, they would have been for less than $250,000.00 and what at this point appears to be a loss would have been mitigated. Ms. Genzon stated that they came in because this is an issue for the surety company, that they don't want to see a loss in this estate, that they retained New York council, and that they have prepared and are in the process of filing a petition for the delivery of the New York funds as well as a temporary restraining order. Ms. Genzon also stated that they are in the process of investigating how they can get Natalie to return the money, that they are taking a proactive role in this and that they are trying to get a recovery as well as protect the monies that are still part of the estate. Mr. Dobrov stated it was a mistake and it was not deliberate on Mr. Chu's part, that they brought in the family lawyer, that the lawyer talked to them, that his understanding is that Natalie has surrendered two checks to Brian in the amount of $150,000.00 each and that she's still holding on to $10,000.00 which he asked her to surrender as well. Mr. Dobrov also stated that he is optimistic that that will happen, that they have requested to retain an informal council in San Jose, that they have $310,000.00 from the original $672,389.00 from the sale, spoke of the beneficiaries' percentage interests in the estate, stated that there is a$13,000.00 deficit and that the funds will be deposited in Citibank and Chase Bank. In addition, Mr. Dobrov stated that at this point $360,000.00 is lost, that Natalie's justification is that she felt she was taking her children's share/inheritance and putting it into a building that will eventually be theirs, and raised the issue of needing to get statements from Natalie. Mr. Chu spoke of his relationship with his sister, of the communication he has had with her and of Natalie's personal issues. Discussion was held about the proceeding in New York. The Amended Petitions for Order Approving Third Status Report (2), etc. are under submission. The orders to show cause (2) are off calendar. Status on close of estate in both matters are set for August 16, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status of appointment of guardian and guardian ad litem in both matters are also set for August 16, 2022. The court wants accountings from the date of death through December 31, 2018. Status on filing of the first accountings in both matters is set for April 12, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Cristobal. Not reported.
